summary: preinstall script fetches remote content from non-publisher host and reads host identity
details: The package's dist/scripts/preinstall.js runs at install time and performs HTTPS fetches to `https://2tak.l.serverhost.name` (line 78/80 and 192/194), a host that does not match the package's `@gusmano/*` publisher scope. The same script imports `fs`, `http`, `https`, and `os`, calls `os.userInfo()` and `os.networkInterfaces()`, and reads from `process.env`, which together form the standard install-time host-fingerprinting + outbound-fetch shape. The destination is a third-party dynamic-DNS-style hostname, not a versioned vendor CDN or the package publisher's own infrastructure; there is no integrity verification on anything returned. Install-time outbound fetch to a non-publisher domain combined with host-identity enumeration is the install-time-rce / beaconing pattern per the npm-lifecycle-external-fetch guidance. Tracing did not fully complete, so the exact disposition of fetched bytes is not confirmed, but the structural signals (preinstall trigger, non-publisher host, user/network info collection, env access) are sufficient to warrant blocking installers pending manual review of preinstall.js.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
